reasoning:
The finding claims stored XSS at line 206 of get_updates_description() where $update['description'] is concatenated into '

' . $update['description'] . '

' without escaping. This concatenation is real, but it is NOT the sink. Both call sites in the admin class (class-favicon-by-realfavicongenerator-admin.php lines 521 and 559) wrap the full return value in esc_html() before echoing: `echo esc_html( $description )`. esc_html() encodes all HTML-significant characters, so any injected script content (and even the

tags added by the function itself) are rendered as literal escaped text, not interpreted HTML. The reachability claim is therefore wrong — the attacker-controlled API data never reaches the DOM as unescaped HTML. The finding is a false positive due to the reviewer missing the esc_html() guards at both rendering sites.
